title: delegation — broadcast channels
description: Two channels — one tenant-plane for delegation lifecycle updates in real time, one platform-plane for impersonation session updates surfaced to the operator's browser (session ends, extensions, forced-terminations should update the operator's UI without a full poll).

channels.authorization: presence — caller must be a member of tenant_id AND hold delegations.viewAny
channels.purpose: Powers the tenant admin's /me/delegations table live-update. Also powers the delegate-received widget in the shell — when a new inbound delegation activates, the delegate's shell shows the 'you can now act as X' banner instantly.

channels.authorization: private — caller must be the PlatformUser with matching id AND hold platform.impersonate
channels.purpose: Powers the impersonation-countdown-timer widget in the platform-admin shell + real-time termination notification. When security force-terminates the operator's session, the operator's browser knows within a second — critical for UX + for the operator to stop typing into a session they no longer have.

notes: Broadcast channels are OPPORTUNISTIC — the tenant / operator UI polls as a fallback (30s). Losing a broadcast never breaks the audit trail; the DB is the source of truth.

There is NO cross-tenant broadcast for impersonation on the tenant plane. The impersonated User does not get a live WebSocket event when their account is impersonated — they get the cannot-opt-out notification (mail + in-app) via the notifications module. Live-broadcasting to the impersonated User would leak operator identity in real time before the audit stream has committed.
